channel.title: Health News, Wellbeing Tips, Diseases, Treatment and Nutrition | The Hindu
channel.description: News and information on medical treatment, diets, cancer, lifestyle diseases, communicable diseases, hospitals, organ donation, blood donation, AIDS, old age, mental health

image.title: Health News, Wellbeing Tips, Diseases, Treatment and Nutrition | The Hindu

item.title: Heatwave plays havoc, exposes Telangana’s fragile health and social systems
President Droupadi Murmu confers Padma Vibhushan on Nageshwar Reddy
item.description: Dr.Reddy, who serves as the chairman of the Asian Institute of Gastroenterology (AIG) in Hyderabad, is among seven persons to receive the Padma Vibhushan this year

item.title: All you need to know about: shingles
item.description: Typically, people over the age of 50, pregnant women, newborns, older persons and those with compromised immune systems are at risk of contracting shingles

item.title: Indian scientists help develop Brain-Computer Interface that enables movement for people who are paralysed
item.description: The participant, after training with a virtual robotic arm, was able to control a real-world robotic arm to perform tasks like picking up blocks, opening a cabinet, and even holding a cup under a water dispenser, demonstrating the potential of BCIs to enable life-changing tasks for individuals with paralysis

item.title: Cancer scares people, breaks them emotionally, financially; its treatment is govt's top priority: Nadda
item.description: More than 26 crore individuals have been screened for oral cancer, 14 crore for breast cancer, and 9 crore for cervical cancer aiding early detection efforts, says Nadda

item.title: Disease surveillance stepped up after Kerala Health department confirms cholera death in Thiruvananthapuram
item.description: Water samples collected from wells for testing

item.title: Smoking is a major risk factor for rheumatoid arthritis
item.description: Speaking at a webinar on “Understanding Arthritis”, they pointed out that smoking is a strong risk factor for rheumatoid arthritis and advocated lifestyle changes, exercises, yoga, and a healthy diet that is low on carbohydrate and sugar but rich in fibre and protein

item.title: NIV shares H5N1 genome data in a public database
item.description: The institute has shared the genome sequence data with the Global Initiative on Sharing All Influenza Data, on April 21, a day aftera report was published in The Hindu about NIV not sharing sequence data before paper publication

item.title: Tremors, seizures, paralysis: this brain disorder is more common than multiple sclerosis - but often goes undiagnosed
item.description: Functional Neurological Disorder (FND) is a condition characterised by real symptoms without visible neurological damage, requiring accurate diagnosis and personalised treatment for effective management

item.title: NIMHANS study identifies blood-based biomarkers for early detection of postpartum depressive symptoms
item.description: Breakthrough study offers hope for early diagnosis and intervention in maternal mental health

item.title: Health policy was working in silos before 2017, govt has made it holistic: Nadda
item.description: Government plans to expand Ayushman Arogya Mandirs to 1.75 lakh, enhancing access to comprehensive primary healthcare and wellness services for communities

item.title: Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam scheme hits the mark by improving hypertension, diabetes control in T.N.
item.description: Tamil Nadu’s MTM scheme has led to better health outcomes, with blood pressure management among adults improving by 17% and blood sugar levels by 16.7%

item.title: Interview | Community participation and digital innovation vital for universal immunisation: UNDP India chief
item.description: UNDP supports India’s immunisation programme, reaching millions annually, with digital platforms and community initiatives for universal coverage

item.title: Telangana had more preventable C-sections among southern States
item.description: At 3.1%, Kerala had the lowest number of preventable C-sections, while Telangana had the highest at 8.4%. Preventable C-sections was 5.4% for Andhra Pradesh, 6.2% for Karnataka, and 7% for Tamil Nad

item.title: Assessing temperature-related deaths in urban heat islands
item.description: In the context of climate change and rapid urbanisation, it is important to understand the broader impact of the urban heat island effect on both heat- and cold-related deaths for urban heat mitigation strategies

item.title: How did WHO nations strike a pandemic deal? | Explained
item.description: What are the highlights of the new agreement? Why was it long overdue? What challenges did countries face during negotiations? How will it impact global cooperation and equity in the next pandemic? Has a balance been struck between global equity and market-based innovation?

item.title: As India’s kidney transplant gap widens, experts call for donor pool expansion
item.description: India faces a growing health emergency as the number of patients with End-Stage Kidney Disease outpaces the country’s capacity for transplants

item.title: Your go-to snacks could be fuelling chronic inflammation – here’s why
item.description: Research shows that diets high in ultra-processed foods are consistently associated with elevated inflammatory markers and increased risk of adverse health outcomes such as cardiometabolic diseases, obesity, Type 2 diabetes, mental health issues, and even higher mortality rates

item.title: Measles: Incidence on the decline in Karnataka, but pockets in BBMP and Ballari record higher number of cases
item.description: Measles and rubella are highly infectious viral diseases that can lead to serious illnesses, lifelong complications, and even death

item.title: Teaching children to eat well must begin in school
item.description: Food literacy is no longer a luxury; the focus must shift from just feeding children to teaching them how to eat well

item.title: 16-year-old girl undergoes robot-assisted heart surgery at Tamil Nadu Government Multi Super Speciality Hospital
item.description: The girl was diagnosed with atrial septal defect, a congenital cardiac defect. The procedure was performed under the Chief Minister’s Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme

item.title: India’s first indigenously developed HPV test kits for cervical cancer screening validated
item.description: The kits could be a major breakthrough in affordable diagnostics for early detection and prevention

item.title: U.S. funding cuts likely to impact long-term research in malaria: Jane Carlton
item.description: Dr. Jane Carlton is a globally recognized leader in malaria genomics and director of the Johns Hopkins Malaria Research Institute. In an email interview, she details the impact of aid suspension and funding cuts on malaria control, discusses the newest breakthroughs in research and warns of climate change potentially increasing malaria cases worldwide says that though India will not see any immediate adverse effect of the US funding cut to the global malaria programme it will, however, impact the national research, training and teaching of the next generation of malaria researchers

item.title: The mosquito effect: how malarial chaos influenced human history
item.description: Once thought to arise from ‘bad air’, the mosquito-borne parasite altered migration and shaped racial hierarchies; the disease continues to afflict approximately 263 million people annually, and with Africa still shouldering a majority of the burden; while deaths have decreased, it still remains a major public health challenge

item.title: Startup Summit-25 | Healthcare industry is in need of technology intervention at all levels: experts
item.description: Panelists recall from their experience the benefit that have accrued to patients and medical professionals

item.title: More than a smile: why orthodontic care for students is imperative
item.description: Studies suggest that 20%-30% of schoolchildren may require orthodontic attention. But treatment is often delayed because of cost, lack of awareness, or stigma. The Virudhunagar model shows the way forward to deal with the problem at the district level

item.title: Experts warn against overuse of last-line antibiotics
item.description: Drugs Controller General of India has been urged to lay down strict pathways for newer antibiotics, so that they are not misused by practitioners

item.title: When it comes to edible oil, finding the right balance is key
item.description: Blending oils such as groundnut, sesame, rice bran, and mustard helps maintain a healthy balance of fats and may reduce the risk of diabetes and metabolic disorders. However, moderate consumption is essential

item.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder and the importance of early diagnosis and support
item.description: Autism doesn’t limit someone’s potential, it simply reflects a different, yet equally valuable way of experiencing life.

item.title: Temperatures soar past 45°C in four Telangana districts, heatwave warning issued
item.description: Telangana heatwave intensifies, with temperatures soaring above 45°C in multiple districts, IMD issues heatwave alert for April 25

item.title: Tamil Nadu bans mayonnaise made from raw eggs for one year
item.description: Mayonnaise is a semi-solid emulsion generally composed of egg yolk, vegetable oil, vinegar and other seasonings served along with food items such as shawarma

item.title: All stress and no play making children anxious, with fragile mental health
item.description: Psychologists and writers are linking the mental health crisis among teenagers to the rising use of digital technologies, a ‘Pandora’s box’ rewiring their brains. The way out, they say, is limiting social media use drastically for children, more unsupervised play, childhood independence and reading

item.title: Indigenously developed HPV test kits for cervical cancer screening launched in Delhi
item.description: Besides the high incidence of cervical cancer owing to its late diagnosis and with consequent poor survival, 25% of global mortality due to cervical cancer occurs in India, said former head of the department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology at AIIMS, Delhi

item.title: Not just DNA, proteins: CCMB team finds lipids also guide evolution
item.description: Scientists’ understanding of lipids is limited to their role as things that hold proteins. A study from Swasti Raychaudhuri’s lab challenges this view

item.title: School-based dance routines reduce the risk of non-communicable diseases among girls, study finds
item.description: According to the study, a 10-minute, high-intensity dance routine during school hours reduced body fat and blood pressure in adolescent girls within 12 weeks, highlighting the effectiveness of school-based health interventions

item.title: Union Health Ministry brings in new competency-based curricula for 10 allied and healthcare professions
item.description: India is skilling its healthcare professionals not just for India, but for the international market, says the Ministry; curricula of professions such as Physiotherapy, Optometry and Dialysis Therapy to see revision

item.title: The rise of ‘text neck’: How phones are reshaping our spines
item.description: Using a smartphone involves a frequent downward gaze, with the head in the forward position for lengthy periods, which can cause neck strain, eventually hastening the process of ageing in the cervical spine

item.title: All you need to know about: anxiety disorders
item.description: Anxiety disorders are the world’s most common mental disorders, says the World Health Organization, with an estimated 301 million people affected as of 2019

item.title: When correcting a young person is perceived as rejection: Understanding how to parent and mentor adolescents
item.description: To truly understand and support the younger generation, we must view their experiences through their lens, rather than solely from an adult’s perspective: good communication, a nurturing environment and guidance towards positive life choices, are essential

item.title: U.S. health officials seek to phase out artificial dyes from food supply
item.description: Officials say they will urge food makers to phase out petroleum-based artificial colours in the nation’s food supply

item.title: Dialysis patients struggle to get treatment in blockaded Gaza; officials say hundreds have died
item.description: Amid Israeli blockade, Gaza dialysis patients struggle to survive due to lack of supplies, and destroyed health care system

item.title: Senior citizens in India grapple with long distances to health facilities, study finds
item.description: The study notes that older adults, on an average, travelled a distance of 14.54 km to seek outpatient services and 43.62 km for inpatient care respectively; those living rurally have to travel far greater distances than their urban counterparts

item.title: Reschedule work hours to mitigate effect of extreme heatwaves, Centre tells States
item.description: Along with special attention for construction and brick kiln workers, the Labour Ministry has recommended adequate drinking water facilities, ventilation and cooling, rest areas, and health check-ups

item.title: Surveillance, R&D innovation and communication are key levers for India to lead the fight against AMR
item.description: India has the tools, the talent, and the urgency to lead the world in curbing antimicrobial resistance. But all scientific efforts need to be unified and communicated to the general public and experts alike, in ways that resonate with them

item.title: Looking back to the strength of a people’s movement against filariasis
item.description: It was a movement in Kerala that first cast the spotlight on filariasis and began measures at several levels to eliminate the disease Kerala’s experiment has shown the route to weed out the disease

item.title: Over 9,400 new HIV cases detected in Telangana in 2024
Blood pressure treatment could lower risk of dementia, clinical trial data suggests
item.description: According to the study, treatment of hypertension was associated with a 15% decrease in dementia incidence and a 16% decrease in cognitive decline over a four-year follow-up period

item.title: When heat is the central paradigm
item.description: On rising temperatures and climate change, India’s air quality, the need for geriatric care services, the landmark agreement on tackling future pandemics, and more

item.title: ‘Poshan Pakhwada’ held for pregnant, lactating women in Visakhapatnam
Indians battle respiratory issues, skin rashes in world’s most polluted town
item.description: Byrnihat’s annual average PM2.5 concentration in 2024 was over 25-times the level recommended by the WHO

item.title: Tiny pacemaker ups the ante on device’s abilities
item.description: The pacemaker is so small that its developers have said it can simply be injected into the heart

item.title: Rising women’s health issues in Hyderabad slums puts spotlight on overworked ASHA workers
Coins, chemicals and more: doctors urge vigilance amid rise in accidental ingestions among kids during summer vacations
item.description: Accidental ingestions of both solids and liquids can be dangerous: doctors advise keeping chemicals well out of reach, carefully supervising children playing with toys, and getting immediate medical help in case a child swallows something they must not

item.title: Science Quiz: On liver health
item.description: How much do you know about your liver and its health? Take this quiz to find out!

item.title: Supreme Court asks man to file suit for damages after he claims disability post COVID-19 first dose
item.description: The petitioner’s counsel said the man was suffering from the adverse effects of immunisation after taking the first dose of COVID-19 vaccine as he developed 100% lower limb disability

item.title: Study links climate change with rising arsenic levels in rice, increasing cancer risks for Asians
item.description: According to the study, rising temperatures and CO2 levels could lead to a substantial increase in arsenic levels in rice, potentially causing tens of millions of cancer cases in Asian countries by 2050

item.title: Pope Francis, first Latin-American to head the Catholic Church, passes away at 88
item.description: Born Jorge Mario Bergoglio, Pope Francis was elected the 266th pontiff on March 13, 2013

item.title: The science behind ‘miracle babies’ born of uterine transplants
item.description: Uterine transplants are now being viewed as a fertility solution for to women who are born without a uterus or women who have undergone a hysterectomy

item.title: Adding community to the equation of elderly care
item.description: By 2050, the elderly population, for the first time, is expected to exceed the population aged 0-15. This underscores the importance of turning the healthcare spotlight on the elderly, with community participation in focus, say experts

item.title: Summer camp boom: Parents in Chennai worry about heat, experts stress need for safety
item.description: The first criteria for most parents when choosing a summer camp has been the timings, with no outdoor activity scheduled during the hot afternoon; experts say camps need to have safety protocols in place

item.title: Doctor, hospital staffer sacked for dragging and assaulting a 77-year-old man in Madhya Pradesh government hospital
item.description: The man was accompanying his 70-year-old wife, a patient; hospital superintendent, who initially claimed the victim was “cutting the queue”, also suspended; FIR filed with charges of criminal intimidation

item.title: Three persons die and 39 fall ill at Woraiyur in Tiruchi
item.description: While residents allege water contamination, civic officials suspect that food contamination during a recent festival might be the cause

item.title: What happened just then? Demystifying the many causes behind syncope
item.description: Syncope is a transient loss of consciousness, but the path to the hospital for it, is as varied as are its causes

item.title: Trials demonstrate safety of stem cell therapy for Parkinson’s
NIV’s unceasing refusal to share vital data before publication
item.description: NIV researchers prioritising paper publication over free and immediate access to genome sequence data and other vital information was once again seen in the case of an 11-year-old child infected with H5N1 in 2021

item.title: Leptospirosis, shigellosis, hepatitis A cases rising in Kerala
item.description: There were 3,525 leptospirosis cases in 2024, up from 2,482 in 2022. In 2024, there were 7,966 hepatitis A cases; the figure was 231 in 2022. The State reported 119 cases of shigella in 2024 and 83 cases in 2022

item.title: On World Liver Day, PM Modi pitches for a fitter India
item.description: Small steps like reducing oil intake can make a big difference. Together, let's build a fitter, healthier India by raising awareness about obesity, PM Modi said in a post on X

item.title: Newborn screening crucial for early detection of primary immunodeficiency, say experts
item.description: Newborn screening tests can detect around 80% of certain primary immunodeficiencies (PI or PIDs) before symptoms appear, enabling early management and potentially improving outcomes

item.title: Doctors seek roll-back of the double shift entrance exam model for NEET PG 2025
item.description: There is lack of transparency and allege disparity between shifts, say doctors

item.title: Understanding irritable bowel syndrome and how to manage it
item.description: Risk factors for IBS include having a family member with IBS, a history of stressful or difficult life events, and having had a severe digestive tract infection

item.title: At this Chennai school, ‘Dr. Dog’ helps children with special needs learn, communicate and build social skills
item.description: Saraswati Kendra uses animal-assisted therapy to help its students who have learning disabilities and autism, develop behavioural skills. Psychologists at the school say the pet is used as a bridge to encourage children to open up

item.title: Are twins allergic to the same things?
item.description: Allergies, whether spring sneezes due to pollen or trouble breathing triggered by a certain food, are caused by a combination of someone’s genes and the environment they live in

item.title: The urgent need to bridge gaps to make dialysis more accessible and affordable for patients
item.description: Diabetes, hypertension and Chronic Kidney Disease of unknown origin are the main causes for the rise in the number of persons requiring dialysis. While some States are well equipped with haemodialysis machines, major gaps exist in others, while access and affordability remain challenges across the country

item.title: 2D Echo technician impersonated a doctor, MBBS doctor functioned as clinical cardiologist at health centre in Telangana’s Suryapet
item.description: IMA complained that several private hospitals operated using medical registration certificates of doctors without their knowledge or consent

item.title: Doyen of Indian angioplasty Mathew Samuel Kalarickal no more
item.description: Dr. Kalarickal performed the first angioplasty in India in 1986, and was also instrumental in setting up and establishing angioplasty facilities in a number of countries in the Asia-Pacific Region

item.title: Making primary health visible, offering accessible and affordable health care
item.description: Local governments should prioritise and locate public health facilities in convenient locations that are accessible with public transportation to increase their visibility

item.title: Indian jails plagued by overcrowding, lack of medical, mental health professionals: report
item.description: Only 25 psychologists available for India’s entire prison population of 5.7 lakh inmates despite doubling in reported mental health illness; 43% vacancy among prison medical officers nationally

item.title: KIMS brings hope to Parkinson’s patients with new treatment
India's air quality way off WHO markers, need to scale up LPG subsidy scheme: Director
Anaphylactic shocks: How your immune system reacts to protect you from the “invaders”
item.description: When you are allergic to a certain food item, and you accidentally consume the same, your immune system reacts to protect you from the “invader”, leading to anaphylaxis or anaphylactic shock

item.title: Indian-origin doctor convicted in U.S. for health care fraud conspiracies
item.description: Indian-origin doctor Neil K. Anand convicted in U.S. for health care fraud, submitting fraudulent claims and unlawfully dispensing controlled substances

item.title: Telangana govt hospital doctors perform liver transplantation on patient suffering from Marfan’s and rare syndrome
item.description: This is a milestone not only for our hospital but also for the global medical community,

item.title: A call to action: Haemophilia patients in India need timely diagnosis, affordable treatment
item.description: Lack of awareness, underdiagnosis, inadequate access to, and unaffordability of treatment remain major barriers in the care of haemophilia patients in India. This needs to change

item.title: Almonds rein in weight, cholesterol and hypertension, confirms study
item.description: Asian Indians with prediabetes can achieve a significant reduction in fasting blood glucose and HbA1C, the study found

item.title: ’Diabetes on Wheels’, an initiative of Kauvery Hospital highlights gaps in early detection and calls for improved awareness
Kerala plans to revise its non-communicable diseases protocol
item.description: The govt. is getting together a multidisciplinary team of experts in medicine, neurology, endocrinology, nephrology, cardiology, nephrology, oncology and public health to deliberate on a new protocol, with an accent on preventing multi-organ complications

item.title: Diet, Sleep, Exercise: A comprehensive guide to a healthy lifestyle
item.description: This e-book sheds light on the various aspects of modern life that are impacting our health, including dietary changes, reduced physical activity, stress, excessive screentime and so on

item.title: All you need to know about: Gluten allergies
item.description: Anyone can have gluten intolerance, though it’s more common in women. Some people are born with gluten intolerance; others develop it later in life.

item.title: Govt bans manufacture, sale of 35 fixed-dose combination drugs
item.description: CDSCO directs States to halt sale of unapproved FDC drugs, emphasizing safety and compliance with drug regulations.

item.title: Doctor, will I ever run again? On life after a heart attack
item.description: Heart attacks are no longer just a problem for the elderly. A lot of young, active people are also at risk because of aspects related to their lifestyle. However, recovery from a heart attack is possible with timely treatment, quality medical care, and healthy lifestyle changes

item.title: WHO countries strike landmark agreement on tackling future pandemics
item.description: Tonight marks a significant milestone in our shared journey towards a safer world, says WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus

item.title: Why are ASHA workers protesting in Kerala? | Explained
item.description: What is the payment structure of Accredited Social Health Activists in Kerala? Does it meet minimum wage?

item.title: Air hostess on ventilator in Gurugram hospital sexually assaulted by staff: Police
item.description: The Gurugram Police said the victim’s statement was recorded before the Magistrate and the matter was under investigation

item.title: Dr. Mohan’s Diabetes Centre launches AI-driven tool for early diabetic retinopathy detection
Telangana prepares for ‘warming planet’ with higher ex-gratia, district-wise heatwave plan
item.description: Officially recognises heatwave as a ‘State-specific disaster’ and raised ex-gratia amount for victims’ families to ₹4 lakh

item.title: Under nPROUD, 21 tonnes of unused or expired drugs from Kozhikode sent for disposal
item.description: Around 14 tonnes of unused or expired drugs collected from Kozhikode Corporation through line collection method, five tonnes through temporary collection, and five tonnes through household collection

item.title: A State-run clinic in Chennai that offers all services to children with neurodevelopmental disorders
item.description: Children are assessed by a team of mental health professionals, and if necessary, treatment is provided

item.title: Rising humidity level makes things very uncomfortable for Chennai residents
item.description: The Regional Meteorological Centre, Chennai, has started providing temperature and humidity forecasts to advise citizens about the impact of heat stress and tips to treat persons affected by sunstroke.

item.title: A Telangana district where 98% of 30+ population were screened for diabetes
item.description: The screening drive, titled ‘Mission Madhumeha’ (diabetes), was conducted from October 2024 to March 31, 2025, to assess the prevalence of diabetes in the 30+ age group

item.title: All you need to know about: Blood pressure
item.description: Measuring blood pressure is important, because high blood pressure is a silent killer -- meaning there are generally no symptoms of it, and it is a major cause of premature death worldwide
